name: Optimizely
what_it_does: Enterprise experimentation and feature management platform providing A/B testing, multivariate testing, feature flags, and personalization. Offers both a full-stack SDK (server-side) and web experimentation.
use_cases: Implement server-side feature flags in agent systems to progressively roll out new tool integrations or model upgrades to a subset of users
A/B test different agent prompt strategies or tool selection algorithms by bucketing users into experiment arms
Control feature availability for agents via remote configuration without code deployments
Use the Optimizely SDK to make real-time feature decisions within agent request handling
Track agent workflow outcomes as conversion events to measure experiment impact
not_for: Simple feature flags for a single-team project — LaunchDarkly or GrowthBook are less complex and cheaper
Teams on a budget — Optimizely is enterprise-priced and not cost-effective for startups
Real-time audience targeting at sub-10ms latency — SDK decisions are fast, but full personalization APIs add overhead
best_when: You need enterprise-grade experimentation with statistical rigor, multi-page funnel analysis, and personalization at scale (10M+ users).
avoid_when: Your team is small, budget-constrained, or needs simple feature toggles — LaunchDarkly, Flagsmith, or GrowthBook are better fits.

auth.notes: SDK uses datafile (JSON config file) for local decisions — no API call at decision time. Management REST API uses personal access tokens. OAuth 2.0 available for management API integrations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing only — estimated $30K-200K+/year based on traffic and features. No public pricing.
pricing.notes: Optimizely does not publish pricing. It is an enterprise SaaS with annual contracts. The open-source SDK is free to use but requires a paid account for the Optimizely platform.

performance.rate_limits: SDK decisions are in-process (<1ms). Management REST API has rate limits not publicly documented.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK returns structured decision objects with variation keys and metadata. REST API returns JSON errors with codes and messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Feature flag decisions are deterministic given same user ID and attributes — same call always returns same result (bucketing is consistent).
known_agent_gotchas: SDK relies on a locally cached 'datafile' (JSON config) that must be refreshed periodically — agents using stale datafiles will not see new experiments or flag changes
User bucketing is based on hashed user ID — agents must use stable, consistent user identifiers across sessions or bucketing will be inconsistent
SDK makes no network calls at decision time (all local) but event tracking (conversions) requires network access and may be dropped if agent network is intermittent
The datafile can be 100KB-1MB+ for large experiment configurations — agents loading it at startup may see initialization delays
Optimizely's free SDK is for server-side feature flags; the web experimentation product (visual editor) uses different APIs entirely

security_score_components.security_notes: SDK uses a public datafile URL (no auth required) for configuration — agents should validate datafile source. Management API tokens should be kept server-side. SOC 2 Type II certified.
